title: Cheapest VPN 2026: Best Value Picks That Don't Sacrifice Privacy
description: The cheapest VPNs in 2026 that still protect your privacy. Real prices, real performance, and which budget picks are worth it.
content:
Cheapest VPN 2026: Best Value Picks That Don't Sacrifice Privacy

The cheapest VPN in 2026 that you can actually trust is Surfshark at around $1.99 per month on a 2-year plan. Private Internet Access comes in just above that at $2.03 per month on a 3-year deal. Both carry independent no-logs audits, work for streaming, and cover unlimited devices. You do not need to spend $10 per month to get solid VPN protection.

Why VPN Pricing Varies So Much

VPN pricing is almost entirely driven by contract length. A provider charging $12.99 per month on a monthly plan might charge $1.99 per month if you commit to two years. This is not a scam. The providers lock in revenue and pass some of that savings to you. The catch: you pay the full 2-year amount upfront, usually around $47-$55.

Monthly VPN plans are genuinely expensive relative to long-term plans. If you need a VPN for more than a few months, a 1- or 2-year plan almost always makes financial sense. Most reputable providers include a 30-day money-back guarantee, so you can exit if the service doesn't work for you.

The Cheapest VPNs in 2026 (Prices Compared)

Here is what the major providers charge on their most affordable long-term plans:

  • Surfshark: ~$1.99/mo on a 2-year plan (unlimited devices)
  • Private Internet Access (PIA): ~$2.03/mo on a 3-year plan (unlimited devices)
  • NordVPN: ~$3.09/mo on a 2-year plan (10 devices)
  • ExpressVPN: ~$6.67/mo on a 1-year plan (8 devices)
  • Mullvad: $5/mo flat, no long-term discount (1 device per session)
  • Proton VPN: Free tier available; paid plans from $3.99/mo

Surfshark and PIA are clear leaders on price. Whether the savings are worth it compared to NordVPN depends on what you actually need from a VPN.

Surfshark: The Cheapest Full-Featured VPN

Surfshark sits at the intersection of low price and genuine capability. At around $1.99 per month on a 2-year plan, it is one of the cheapest VPNs available from a provider that has independently audited its no-logs policy (Deloitte, 2023).

Unlimited simultaneous connections is the headline feature. One Surfshark subscription covers every device in your household, something that NordVPN and ExpressVPN do not offer at any price. If you have a household with multiple phones, laptops, tablets, and smart TVs, this matters.

Server count sits at 3,200+ servers across 100 countries. That is smaller than NordVPN's 6,400+ server fleet, but in practice, most users connect to a handful of countries. Server availability in those countries is comparable.

Speed is good on nearby servers, slightly behind NordVPN on distant connections. For streaming Netflix, Disney+, and BBC iPlayer, Surfshark is reliable. The kill switch and DNS leak protection are both present and on by default.

Where Surfshark falls short: the apps can feel slightly less polished than NordVPN's, and customer support response times lag during peak hours. Neither is a dealbreaker at this price point. For a detailed comparison, see our Surfshark review 2026.

Private Internet Access: Proven No-Logs at Rock-Bottom Price

PIA's most compelling selling point is not its price but its court record. The company has been subpoenaed twice by US law enforcement, and twice it produced no user data because it had none to hand over. This is a real-world test of a no-logs policy, which is more convincing than any audit alone.

At around $2.03 per month on a 3-year plan, PIA is competitive with Surfshark. The 3-year commitment is longer than most people want, but PIA offers 1- and 2-year options at slightly higher prices. It also offers a monthly plan for those who only need short-term coverage.

PIA has one of the largest server networks of any VPN: 35,000+ servers across 84 countries. For users who care about having many server options, PIA is hard to beat. The apps are functional, the WireGuard support is solid, and the split tunneling is more customizable than most competitors.

The US jurisdiction is a concern for some users. PIA is headquartered in the United States, which is part of the Five Eyes intelligence alliance. The court-proven no-logs policy is the main reason this does not disqualify PIA, but users with high threat models may prefer a provider in a more neutral jurisdiction like Switzerland (Proton VPN) or Sweden (Mullvad).

NordVPN: Best Value at the Premium Tier

NordVPN at $3.09 per month is not the cheapest option, but it earns a place in a budget comparison because it delivers significantly more for a relatively small price increase over Surfshark and PIA.

The RAM-only server infrastructure means no data can persist between sessions. The NordLynx protocol (WireGuard-based) is among the fastest available. Threat Protection Pro blocks malware and trackers at the network level. Meshnet creates private encrypted networks between your own devices for free.

If your budget is flexible enough to stretch from $2 to $3 per month, NordVPN gives you measurably better speed on distant servers, a larger network, and more advanced features. Read our NordVPN review 2026 for the full breakdown.

Proton VPN Free: The Best Free Option

Proton VPN is the only free VPN worth recommending without caveats. Most free VPNs have a business model that depends on monetizing your data, which defeats the purpose of using a VPN. Proton VPN's free tier is funded by paid subscribers, not by selling your browsing history.

What the free plan gives you: unlimited data, servers in three countries (US, Netherlands, Japan), and a verified no-logs policy from a Swiss-based provider with a track record in privacy advocacy (Proton also runs ProtonMail). What it does not give you: fast speeds, a choice of servers, access to streaming platforms, or priority support.

For basic privacy protection on a tight budget, Proton VPN Free is the right answer. For anything more demanding, including streaming, gaming, or consistent speeds, a paid plan is worth the money even at budget-tier pricing.

What to Look For in a Cheap VPN

Not all cheap VPNs are equal. These are the things that separate a trustworthy budget VPN from one that could harm you:

  • Independent no-logs audit: An audit by a credible firm (Deloitte, PricewaterhouseCoopers, KPMG) is the minimum standard for trusting a provider's privacy claims. Without one, the no-logs claim is unverifiable.
  • Transparent ownership: Some cheap VPNs are owned by companies with questionable privacy records or opaque ownership structures. Check who actually owns the provider before signing up.
  • Kill switch by default: If the VPN drops, your traffic should not leak unencrypted. A kill switch that cuts your internet connection on VPN failure is a basic safety feature.
  • DNS leak protection: Your DNS queries should route through the VPN tunnel, not through your ISP's DNS servers. Test this at dnsleaktest.com after connecting to confirm.
  • WireGuard support: WireGuard is faster and more efficient than older protocols like OpenVPN. Any modern VPN should support it.

VPNs to Avoid at Any Price

A handful of free and ultra-cheap VPNs have documented histories of logging and selling user data, injecting ads into browsers, or using subscriber devices as exit nodes for others' traffic without disclosure. Among the worst offenders historically: Hola VPN (turned user devices into a botnet), Betternet (logged and sold data per their own privacy policy), and Super VPN (exposed a 100M-user database). None of these are worth using at any price.

If a VPN is completely free and does not have a clear explanation of how it funds itself, assume your data is the product.

Monthly vs Long-Term Plans: The Real Cost Comparison

Here is what Surfshark actually costs depending on the plan you choose:

  • Monthly: ~$15.45/mo
  • 1-year: ~$2.99/mo (total ~$35.88 billed annually)
  • 2-year: ~$1.99/mo (total ~$53.28 billed every 2 years)

The 2-year plan is roughly 87% cheaper per month than paying monthly. For most users who know they will use a VPN long-term, the 2-year plan is the obvious choice. If you are unsure, the 30-day money-back guarantee covers you if you change your mind.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the cheapest VPN in 2026?

Surfshark is the cheapest full-featured VPN in 2026 at around $1.99 per month on a 2-year plan. Private Internet Access is similarly priced at $2.03 per month on a 3-year deal. Both include unlimited devices, no-logs policies, and WireGuard support.

Are cheap VPNs safe?

Some are, some are not. Surfshark, Private Internet Access, and NordVPN all offer budget pricing on long plans and have independent audits backing their privacy claims. Truly free VPNs often monetize your data instead, which makes them unsafe for privacy purposes.

Is NordVPN cheap?

NordVPN costs around $3.09 per month on a 2-year plan. Not the cheapest, but competitive for a premium VPN. Surfshark and Private Internet Access undercut it on price while remaining credible providers.

What is the cheapest VPN with no logs?

Surfshark has a no-logs policy audited by Deloitte and costs around $1.99 per month on a 2-year plan. Private Internet Access has a court-tested no-logs policy and costs around $2.03 per month on a 3-year plan.

Can I get a VPN for free?

Proton VPN Free is the most trustworthy free VPN. No data cap, verified no-logs, servers in three countries. Speeds are slower and server choice is limited. For more demanding use, a paid budget plan at $2-3/mo is worth the upgrade.

Is Surfshark better than NordVPN?

Surfshark is cheaper and has unlimited simultaneous connections; NordVPN is faster and has a larger network. For the lowest price without major trade-offs, Surfshark wins. For maximum speed and features, NordVPN is the better choice.

How do I get the cheapest VPN price?

Long-term plans (2-3 years) always offer the lowest per-month cost. A 2-year Surfshark plan at $1.99/mo is roughly 60% cheaper than paying month-to-month at $15.45/mo. Black Friday and seasonal promotions sometimes push prices even lower.
